prompt: List all skills and their descriptions. I need to know about QA, ship, and testing skills.
content:
# All gstack Skills and Their Descriptions

## 📋 Complete Skill Inventory

### Strategy & Planning
| Skill | Role | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **`/office-hours`** | YC Office Hours | Six forcing questions that reframe your product before writing code. Pushes back on framing, challenges premises, generates implementation alternatives. Feeds design doc into downstream skills. |
| **`/plan-ceo-review`** | CEO / Founder | Rethink the problem; find the "10-star product" hiding inside the request. Four modes: Expansion, Selective Expansion, Hold Scope, Reduction. |
| **`/plan-eng-review`** | Eng Manager | Lock in architecture, data flow, diagrams, edge cases, and tests. "Forces hidden assumptions into the open." |
| **`/plan-design-review`** | Senior Designer | Rates each design dimension 0-10, explains what a 10 looks like, edits the plan. AI Slop detection with interactive questions. |
| **`/plan-devex-review`** | DX Lead | Interactive DX review exploring developer personas, TTHW benchmarks, friction points. 20-45 forcing questions across three modes. |
| **`/autoplan`** | Review Pipeline | One command running CEO → design → eng review automatically. "Surfaces only taste decisions for your approval." |

### Design & Mockups
| Skill | Role | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **`/design-consultation`** | Design Partner | Builds a complete design system from scratch. Researches landscape, proposes creative risks, generates realistic product mockups. |
| **`/design-shotgun`** | Design Explorer | Generates 4-6 AI mockup variants, opens a comparison board in your browser, collects feedback, and iterates. Taste memory learns preferences over time. |
| **`/design-html`** | Design Engineer | Turns mockups into production HTML using Pretext computed text layout (~30KB, zero deps). Detects React/Svelte/Vue. "The output is shippable, not a demo." |
| **`/design-review`** | Designer Who Codes | Same audit as `/plan-design-review` but then fixes what it finds with atomic commits and before/after screenshots. |

### Code Review & Quality
| Skill | Role | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **`/review`** | Staff Engineer | Finds bugs that "pass CI but blow up in production." Auto-fixes obvious issues, flags completeness gaps. |
| **`/codex`** | Second Opinion | Independent code review from OpenAI Codex CLI. Three modes: pass/fail gate, adversarial challenge, and open consultation. Cross-model analysis when both `/review` and `/codex` have run. |
| **`/investigate`** | Debugger | Systematic root-cause debugging. "Iron Law: no fixes without investigation." Traces data flow, tests hypotheses, stops after 3 failed fixes. |
| **`/cso`** | Chief Security Officer | OWASP Top 10 + STRIDE threat model. "Zero-noise: 17 false positive exclusions, 8/10+ confidence gate." Each finding includes a concrete exploit scenario. |

### QA & Testing (Your Focus Area)
| Skill | Role | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **`/qa`** | QA Lead | Tests your app in a real browser, finds bugs, fixes them with atomic commits, generates regression tests, and re-verifies. The README says this was "a massive unlock" — it enabled scaling from 6 to 12 parallel workers. Claude Code says "I SEE THE ISSUE" and then fixes, generates a regression test, and verifies. |
| **`/qa-only`** | QA Reporter | "Same methodology as /qa but report only. Pure bug report without code changes." |
| **`/devex-review`** | DX Tester | Live developer experience audit. Actually tests onboarding: navigates docs, tries the getting-started flow, times TTHW, screenshots errors. Compares against `/plan-devex-review` scores — described as "the boomerang that shows if your plan matched reality." |

### Ship & Release (Your Focus Area)
| Skill | Role | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **`/ship`** | Release Engineer | Syncs main, runs tests, audits coverage, pushes, opens a PR. "Bootstraps test frameworks if you don't have one." Every `/ship` run produces a coverage audit. Auto-invokes `/document-release`. |
| **`/land-and-deploy`** | Release Engineer | Merges the PR, waits for CI and deploy, verifies production health. "One command from 'approved' to 'verified in production.'" |
| **`/spec`** | Spec Author | Turns vague intent into a precise, executable spec in five phases. Codex quality gate blocks below 7/10. `--execute` spawns a fresh claude worktree; `/ship` auto-closes the source issue on merge. |

### Post-Deploy & Monitoring
| Skill | Role | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **`/canary`** | SRE | Post-deploy monitoring loop. Watches for console errors, performance regressions, and page failures. |
| **`/benchmark`** | Performance Engineer | Baselines page load times, Core Web Vitals, and resource sizes. Compares before/after on every PR. |

### Documentation
| Skill | Role | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **`/document-release`** | Technical Writer | Reads every doc file, cross-references the diff, updates everything that drifted. "Catches stale READMEs automatically." Builds a Diataxis coverage map visible in the PR body. |
| **`/document-generate`** | Documentation Author | Generates missing docs from scratch using the Diataxis framework. Researches the codebase first. Can run standalone or be chained from `/document-release`. |

### Browser & Device Testing
| Skill | Role | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **`/browse`** | QA Engineer | "Give the agent eyes. Real Chromium browser, real clicks, real screenshots. ~100ms per command." |
| **`/open-gstack-browser`** | Browser Launcher | Launches GStack Browser with sidebar, anti-bot stealth, auto model routing (Sonnet for actions, Opus for analysis), one-click cookie import, and Claude Code integration. |
| **`/setup-browser-cookies`** | Session Manager | Imports cookies from your real browser (Chrome, Arc, Brave, Edge) into the headless session for testing authenticated pages. |
| **`/ios-qa`** | iOS Tester | Drives a real iPhone over USB CoreDevice via embedded StateServer in the app. Capability-tier allowlist (observe/interact/mutate/restore), per-device session lock, audit log. |
| **`/ios-fix`** | iOS Bug Fixer | iOS bug-fix loop. |
| **`/ios-design-review`** | iOS Designer | "Designer's-eye HIG audit" for iOS apps. |
| **`/ios-clean`** | iOS Cleanup | Debug-bridge cleanup. |
| **`/ios-sync`** | iOS Sync | Accessor resync for iOS QA. |

### Agents & Coordination
| Skill | Role | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **`/pair-agent`** | Multi-Agent Coordinator | Shares your browser with any AI agent. Works with OpenClaw, Hermes, Codex, Cursor. Each agent gets its own tab. "The first time AI agents from different vendors can coordinate through a shared browser with real security." |
| **`/retro`** | Eng Manager | Team-aware weekly retro with per-person breakdowns, shipping streaks, test health trends, growth opportunities. `/retro global` runs across all projects and AI tools. |
| **`/learn`** | Memory | Manages what gstack learned across sessions. Review, search, prune, and export project-specific patterns. "Learnings compound across sessions so gstack gets smarter on your codebase over time." |

### Safety & Guardrails
| Skill | Role | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **`/careful`** | Safety Guardrails | Warns before destructive commands (rm -rf, DROP TABLE, force-push). Say "be careful" to activate. |
| **`/freeze`** | Edit Lock | Restricts file edits to one directory to prevent accidental changes outside scope while debugging. |
| **`/guard`** | Full Safety | `/careful` + `/freeze` in one command. Maximum safety for prod work. |
| **`/unfreeze`** | Unlock | Removes the `/freeze` boundary. |

### Setup & Maintenance
| Skill | Role | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **`/setup-deploy`** | Deploy Configurator | One-time setup for `/land-and-deploy`. Detects your platform, production URL, and deploy commands. |
| **`/setup-gbrain`** | GBrain Onboarding | From zero to running gbrain in under 5 minutes with four paths (Supabase auto-provision, existing URL, PGLite local, remote MCP). |
| **`/sync-gbrain`** | Brain Sync | Re-indexes repo code into gbrain. Idempotent and safe to re-run. |
| **`/gstack-upgrade`** | Self-Updater | Upgrades gstack to latest. Detects global vs vendored install, syncs both, shows changelog. |

### Publishing & Diagrams
| Skill | Role | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **`/make-pdf`** | Publisher | Markdown in, publication-quality document out. Mermaid and excalidraw fences render as vector diagrams fully offline. Supports `--to html` and `--to docx`. |
| **`/diagram`** | Diagram Maker | "English in, editable diagram out." Emits mermaid source, `.excalidraw` file, and rendered SVG/PNG. |

---

## Deep Dive: QA, Ship, and Testing Skills

### `/qa` — The QA Lead
This is the flagship testing skill. It opens a **real browser**, clicks through your flows, finds bugs, fixes them with atomic commits, auto-generates **regression tests** for every fix, and re-verifies. The README highlights this as a major productivity unlock — it enabled the author to scale from 6 to 12 parallel workers. The workflow is: see an issue → fix it → write a regression test → verify. Every QA fix leaves behind a permanent test.

### `/qa-only` — QA Without Code Changes
Same methodology as `/qa` but produces a pure **bug report** with no code changes. Useful when you want a human to review findings before applying fixes, or when you need a third party to validate.

### `/ship` — The Release Engineer
This skill handles the entire release pipeline: syncs main, runs tests, audits coverage, pushes changes, and opens a PR. It's notable for being **test-aware** — if your project has no test framework, it bootstraps one from scratch. Every `/ship` run produces a coverage audit. It now **auto-invokes** `/document-release` so docs stay current without an extra command. Also integrates with `/spec`: running `/spec --execute` will auto-close the source issue on merge when `/ship` completes.

### `/canary` — Post-Deploy Monitoring
After deployment, this acts as your SRE, watching for console errors, performance regressions, and page failures in a monitoring loop.

### `/benchmark` — Performance Testing
Baselines page load times, Core Web Vitals, and resource sizes, then compares before/after on every PR — integrated with the ship workflow.

### Test Coverage Philosophy
The README states: "100% test coverage is the goal — tests make vibe coding safe instead of yolo coding." The system is built so every `/qa` bug fix generates a regression test, and `/ship` includes a coverage audit and framework bootstrapping for projects without existing tests.
